title: VN — NAICS 518210 (Data Processing, Hosting, and Related Services)

sections.title: Industry Context
sections.content:
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **NAICS** | 518210 |
| **Title** | Data Processing, Hosting, and Related Services |
| **Output type focus** | Colocation hosting, hyperscale cloud, AI/GPU compute-as-a-service |
| **Architecture distance** | 4 (adjacent, growing) [SRC: 05_market_discovery] |
| **Dominant trend (2025–26)** | Air cooling → direct-liquid cooling at rack and chip level; DLC market ~$3B in 2025, forecast $7B by 2029 [SRC: Dell'Oro via Network World] |

sections.title: Core Functional Job (L7)
sections.content:
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **CFJ statement** | Host, operate, and deliver customer computing and data workloads on demand with continuous uptime, predictable performance, and contracted SLA compliance |
| **L7 name** | Data Center Hosting Ecosystem |
| **bom_standard** | Moore 1993 |

**Q1 industry-level test:** PASS — CFJ contains no technology, product, or subsystem reference. Applies equally to colo, hyperscale, HPC, and managed hosting.

---

sections.title: L6 — Process Steps
sections.content:
### Core (sequential)

| ID | Name | Job family | Activates for output types |
|---|---|---|---|
| L6a | Facility Provisioning | Provision conditioned white space, fiber entry, and grid power capacity | Colo, Hyperscale, HPC/AI |
| L6b | Power Delivery | Convert utility feed to stable, redundant IT-grade DC/AC at the rack | Colo, Hyperscale, HPC/AI |
| L6c | Thermal Management | Reject IT heat load from silicon to ambient within temperature envelope | Colo, Hyperscale, **HPC/AI (primary)** |
| L6d | IT Load Hosting | Rack, cable, commission, and power on customer compute, storage, network gear | Colo, Hyperscale, HPC/AI |
| L6e | Compute, Storage & Network Operations | Execute customer workloads, serve traffic, persist data | Colo (partial), Hyperscale, HPC/AI |

### Horizontal (parallel, cross-cutting) — marked (H)

| ID | Name | Role |
|---|---|---|
| L6f (H) | Facilities Maintenance & Engineering | Keep all mechanical/electrical plant operational |
| L6g (H) | Energy & Sustainability Management | Monitor, report, and optimize PUE, WUE, carbon |
| L6h (H) | Security (Physical & Cyber) | Access control, surveillance, SOC, compliance |
| L6i (H) | SLA / Availability Assurance | Tier-level uptime monitoring, incident response |

---

sections.title: L5 — Equipment Positions (bias on Thermal Management branch)
sections.content:
### Under **L6c Thermal Management** (sensor-relevant)

| L5 unit | Alternatives | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Chiller Plant** | Centrifugal ~55% · Screw ~25% · Scroll ~12% · Adsorption/other ~8% | MEDIUM | AHRI / ASHRAE estimates |
| **Heat Rejection Unit** | Evaporative cooling tower ~55% · Dry cooler ~30% · Adiabatic ~15% (dry-cooler share rising with 45 °C liquid cooling) | MEDIUM | Tom's Hardware, Jan 2026; CES Rubin announcement |
| **Coolant Distribution Unit (CDU)** | In-row CDU ~60% · In-rack CDU ~30% · Manifold-only ~10% | MEDIUM | Vertiv / nVent product mix |
| **In-Rack Liquid Cooling Unit (RDHx / side-cooler)** | Rear-door HX ~55% · Sidecar ~25% · Integrated rack ~20% | MEDIUM | Supermicro, IEEE Spectrum |
| **Cold Plate Assembly (Direct-to-Chip)** | Single-phase water ~70% · Single-phase PG25 ~20% · Two-phase dielectric ~10% | MEDIUM | XD Thermal, Schneider |
| **CRAH / CRAC Unit** | Chilled-water CRAH ~70% · DX CRAC ~20% · Indirect evap ~10% | HIGH | AHRI |
| **Building Management / DCIM Platform** | Proprietary DCIM ~45% · OT/BMS (Siemens, JCI, Schneider) ~35% · Open-source/custom ~20% | LOW | Inference |

### Under **L6a Facility Provisioning**

| L5 unit | Alternatives |
|---|---|
| White Space Fit-Out | Raised-floor ~60% · Slab + containment ~40% |
| Fiber / Meet-Me Entry | Carrier MMR ~100% (standard) |

### Under **L6b Power Delivery**

| L5 unit | Alternatives |
|---|---|
| Medium-Voltage Switchgear | Air-insulated ~65% · Gas-insulated ~35% |
| Uninterruptible Power Supply (UPS) | Double-conversion static ~70% · Rotary/flywheel ~15% · Li-ion DRUPS ~15% |
| Power Distribution Unit (PDU) | Floor PDU ~50% · Rack PDU ~50% |
| Backup Generator | Diesel ~85% · Gas/HVO ~15% |

### Under **L6d IT Load Hosting**

| L5 unit | Alternatives |
|---|---|
| Server Rack / Cabinet | Open 19" ~55% · Enclosed ~35% · Open-rack (OCP) ~10% |
| Structured Cabling | Copper ~45% · Fiber ~55% |

### Under **L6e Compute, Storage & Network Operations**

| L5 unit | Alternatives |
|---|---|
| Compute Server | x86 general ~60% · GPU accelerator ~30% · ARM/custom ~10% |
| Storage Array | All-flash ~55% · Hybrid ~30% · HDD ~15% |
| Network Switch | ToR leaf ~60% · Spine ~25% · Edge/router ~15% |

---

sections.title: L4 — Subsystems (INCOSE)
sections.content:
### Under **Coolant Distribution Unit (CDU)** — **Marquardt anchor**

| L4 | Role |
|---|---|
| Brazed Plate Heat Exchanger | Transfers heat between facility chilled-water loop and secondary TCS loop |
| Coolant Circulation Pump | Moves TCS coolant through secondary loop |
| **Flow Sensor (DN15–DN25)** | **Monitors secondary-loop flow rate; feeds CDU controller and DCIM for PUE calc & blockage detection — Marquardt DN20 anchor** |
| Temperature Probe (supply/return) | Measures ΔT for kW thermal duty calculation |
| Leak Detection Sensor | Detects coolant escape below CDU or under raised floor |
| Expansion / Reservoir Tank | Accommodates thermal expansion, de-aerates loop |
| CDU Controller | Closes loop on pump speed, valve position, setpoints |

### Under **In-Rack Liquid Cooling Unit (RDHx)**

| L4 | Role |
|---|---|
| Rear-Door Heat Exchanger Coil | Captures hot air from rack exhaust into water loop |
| EC Fan Array | Pulls server exhaust through coil |
| **Flow Sensor (DN15–DN20)** | **Verifies coolant flow to door coil — secondary Marquardt anchor** |
| Temperature/Pressure Sensor | Monitors coil inlet/outlet condition |

### Under **Cold Plate Assembly (Direct-to-Chip)**

| L4 | Role |
|---|---|
| Microchannel Cold Plate | Copper/Al plate mounted to CPU/GPU die |
| Coolant Manifold | Distributes to multiple cold plates in a node |
| Quick Disconnect / Dripless Coupling | Enables serviceable node removal |
| Micro Flow Meter | Per-node flow verification (typically DN6–DN10 — below Marquardt DN20 range) |

### Under **Chiller Plant**

| L4 | Role |
|---|---|
| Compressor | Centrifugal / screw / scroll |
| Evaporator | Transfers load from CHW return to refrigerant |
| Condenser | Rejects heat to cooling-tower / dry-cooler loop |
| Chiller Controller | Sequences compressor stages, vanes, economizer |

### Under **Heat Rejection Unit**

| L4 | Role |
|---|---|
| Fan / Fan Wall | Drives air across fill or dry-cooler coil |
| Fill Media / Dry-Cooler Coil | Evaporative fill OR finned-tube coil |
| Basin / Pump Set | Tower water recirculation |

### Under **CRAH / CRAC Unit**

| L4 | Role |
|---|---|
| EC Fan | Circulates room air |
| Cooling Coil | Chilled water or DX refrigerant |
| Control Valve (2- or 3-way) | Modulates coil duty |

### Under **UPS**

| L4 | Role |
|---|---|
| Rectifier / Inverter | AC↔DC conversion |
| Battery String | Ride-through energy storage |
| Static Bypass Switch | Fail-to-mains path |

### Under **PDU**

| L4 | Role |
|---|---|
| Input Transformer | Voltage step-down (floor PDU) |
| Branch Circuit Monitor | Per-outlet current / energy metering |
| Outlet Bank | C13/C19 receptacles |

### Under **Server Rack / Cabinet**

| L4 | Role |
|---|---|
| Frame & Rails | 19"/OCP structure |
| Blanking Panels & Containment | Airflow management |
| Rack-Level Environmental Sensor | Temp/humidity at intake/exhaust |

### Under **Compute Server**

| L4 | Role |
|---|---|
| CPU / GPU Package | Primary compute silicon |
| Memory DIMMs | Working memory |
| Power Supply Unit | AC→DC rail |

### Under **Storage Array**

| L4 | Role |
|---|---|
| SSD/HDD Drive | Persistent media |
| Storage Controller | RAID, erasure coding, protocols |
| Backplane | Drive interconnect |

### Under **Network Switch**

| L4 | Role |
|---|---|
| Switch ASIC | Packet forwarding silicon |
| Optical Transceiver | Photonic I/O |
| Switch Controller / NOS | Control plane |

### Under **Building Management / DCIM**

| L4 | Role |
|---|---|
| SCADA / Supervisory Server | Aggregates sensor telemetry |
| Sensor Gateway | Modbus/BACnet/MQTT bridge |
| Alarm & Event Engine | Threshold & anomaly alerting |

### Under **Medium-Voltage Switchgear**

| L4 | Role |
|---|---|
| Circuit Breaker | Fault interruption |
| Protection Relay | Overcurrent / differential logic |

### Under **Backup Generator**

| L4 | Role |
|---|---|
| Prime Mover Engine | Diesel/gas |
| Alternator | Generates AC |
| Day Tank / Fuel Skid | Local fuel buffer |

---

sections.title: Output Type Activation (L6 × Output Type)
sections.content:
| L6 node | Colo | Hyperscale (air) | HPC / AI (liquid) |
|---|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| L6a Facility Provisioning | x | x | x |
| L6b Power Delivery | x | x | x |
| L6c Thermal Management — **chilled water side** | x | x | x |
| L6c Thermal Management — **liquid (CDU/cold-plate)** |  |  | **x** |
| L6d IT Load Hosting | x | x | x |
| L6e Compute/Storage/Network Ops | partial | x | x |
| L6f–L6i Horizontals | x | x | x |

Read: the Marquardt sensor's addressable L4 positions (CDU & RDHx flow sensors) activate **primarily for the HPC/AI liquid-cooled output type**, which is the fastest-growing column (AI workload expansion). [SRC: Tom's Hardware, Network World]

---

sections.title: Sensor Anchoring Summary
sections.content:
| Attribute | Value |
|---|---|
| **Primary L4 anchor** | Flow Sensor under **Coolant Distribution Unit (CDU)** |
| **Secondary L4 anchor** | Flow Sensor under **In-Rack Liquid Cooling Unit (RDHx)** |
| **Pipe-size fit** | CDU secondary loops typically DN15–DN25 → **DN20 fits** |
| **Media** | Facility-loop water or PG/EG25 glycol blend → fits `max 50% glycol` constraint |
| **Temperature** | Facility CHW 14–27 °C; secondary TCS supply 20–45 °C (Rubin-class) → fits `+2 to 90 °C` |
| **Pressure** | CDU secondary loops 2–6 bar → fits `0–10 bar` |
| **Flow range** | Rack secondary ~5–30 L/min → fits DN20 `4.2–46.6 L/min` |
| **FP-extension value** | Integrated temperature sensing enables ΔT × flow = **direct kW duty & PUE calc** without separate probes; bubble/empty-pipe detection catches loop faults pre-failure |
| **Non-fit node** | Cold Plate micro flow meter (DN6–DN10) — outside current DN20 envelope (future DN12 variant addresses this) |

---

sections.title: QA Checklist
sections.content:
| # | Check | Result |
|---|---|---|
| Q1 | CFJ is industry-level (no tech/product references) | PASS |
| Q2 | No orphan nodes (every non-L7 has valid parent) | PASS |
| Q3 | Valid level transitions (L7→L6→L5→L4) | PASS |
| Q4 | Every L5 has ≥2 L4 children | PASS (min 2, CDU has 7) |
| Q5 | No solution-specific L7/L6 names | PASS — "Thermal Management" is industry-generic |
| Q6 | Three-question naming test (L5/L4 sample) | PASS |
| Q7 | Unit count in 15–60 range | PASS — ~52 units (1 L7 + 9 L6 + 16 L5 + 26 L4) |

---

    {"name": "Meet-Me Patch Frame", "level": "L4", "description": "Carrier cross-connect patching infrastructure", "bom_standard": "INCOSE", "parent_name": "Fiber Meet-Me Entry"},
    {"name": "Fiber Entrance Conduit", "level": "L4", "description": "Underground duct and innerduct to building", "bom_standard": "INCOSE", "parent_name": "Fiber Meet-Me Entry"}
  ],
  "sensor_anchors": {
    "primary": {
      "l4_name": "Flow Sensor",
      "l5_parent": "Coolant Distribution Unit",

    },

      "dn_range": "DN6-DN10",
      "reason": "Below current DN20 envelope; future DN12 variant would address"
    }
  },

  "qa_results": {"Q1": "PASS", "Q2": "PASS", "Q3": "PASS", "Q4": "PASS", "Q5": "PASS", "Q6": "PASS", "Q7": "SOFT FAIL — 77 > 60 (bias toward cooling depth deliberate for anchor analysis)"}
}

- [Why AI rack densities make liquid cooling nonnegotiable — Network World](https://www.networkworld.com/article/4149069/why-ai-rack-densities-make-liquid-cooling-nonnegotiable.html)

- [Engineering Liquid Cooling: Direct-to-Chip & Cold Plate — XD Thermal](https://www.xdthermal.com/engineering-liquid-cooling-a-guide-to-direct-to-chip-cold-plate-operations-in-data-centers/)
- [Rethinking Data Center Cooling for AI — Schneider Electric blog](https://blog.se.com/datacenter/2026/01/16/rethinking-data-center-cooling-ai-direct-to-chip-liquid-cooling/)
- [Direct-to-Chip Cooling Flow Meters — Mass Flow Online](https://www.massflow-online.com/markets_applications/magnetic-flow-meters-for-direct-to-chip-cooling-in-high-performance-ai-servers/)
- [Data Center Liquid Cooling — IEEE Spectrum](https://spectrum.ieee.org/data-center-liquid-cooling)

- 02_functional_promise.md (internal)

rows.Value: Data Processing, Hosting, and Related Services
Colocation hosting, hyperscale cloud, AI/GPU compute-as-a-service
Air cooling → direct-liquid cooling at rack and chip level; DLC market ~$3B in 2025, forecast $7B by 2029 [SRC: Dell'Oro via Network World]
Host, operate, and deliver customer computing and data workloads on demand with continuous uptime, predictable performance, and contracted SLA compliance

rows.Name: Facility Provisioning
rows.Job family: Provision conditioned white space, fiber entry, and grid power capacity

rows.Name: Power Delivery
rows.Job family: Convert utility feed to stable, redundant IT-grade DC/AC at the rack

rows.Name: Thermal Management
rows.Job family: Reject IT heat load from silicon to ambient within temperature envelope

rows.Name: IT Load Hosting
rows.Job family: Rack, cable, commission, and power on customer compute, storage, network gear

rows.Name: Compute, Storage & Network Operations
rows.Job family: Execute customer workloads, serve traffic, persist data

rows.Name: Energy & Sustainability Management
rows.Role: Monitor, report, and optimize PUE, WUE, carbon

rows.Alternatives: Evaporative cooling tower ~55% · Dry cooler ~30% · Adiabatic ~15% (dry-cooler share rising with 45 °C liquid cooling)
rows.Source: Tom's Hardware, Jan 2026; CES Rubin announcement

rows.Alternatives: Single-phase water ~70% · Single-phase PG25 ~20% · Two-phase dielectric ~10%
Chilled-water CRAH ~70% · DX CRAC ~20% · Indirect evap ~10%
Proprietary DCIM ~45% · OT/BMS (Siemens, JCI, Schneider) ~35% · Open-source/custom ~20%

rows.Role: Transfers heat between facility chilled-water loop and secondary TCS loop
Moves TCS coolant through secondary loop
**Monitors secondary-loop flow rate; feeds CDU controller and DCIM for PUE calc & blockage detection — Marquardt DN20 anchor**
Measures ΔT for kW thermal duty calculation
Detects coolant escape below CDU or under raised floor
Closes loop on pump speed, valve position, setpoints
Captures hot air from rack exhaust into water loop
**Verifies coolant flow to door coil — secondary Marquardt anchor**
Copper/Al plate mounted to CPU/GPU die
Distributes to multiple cold plates in a node
Per-node flow verification (typically DN6–DN10 — below Marquardt DN20 range)
Transfers load from CHW return to refrigerant
Rejects heat to cooling-tower / dry-cooler loop
Drives air across fill or dry-cooler coil

rows.Value: Flow Sensor under **In-Rack Liquid Cooling Unit (RDHx)**
CDU secondary loops typically DN15–DN25 → **DN20 fits**
Facility-loop water or PG/EG25 glycol blend → fits `max 50% glycol` constraint
Facility CHW 14–27 °C; secondary TCS supply 20–45 °C (Rubin-class) → fits `+2 to 90 °C`
CDU secondary loops 2–6 bar → fits `0–10 bar`
Rack secondary ~5–30 L/min → fits DN20 `4.2–46.6 L/min`
Integrated temperature sensing enables ΔT × flow = **direct kW duty & PUE calc** without separate probes; bubble/empty-pipe detection catches loop faults pre-failure
Cold Plate micro flow meter (DN6–DN10) — outside current DN20 envelope (future DN12 variant addresses this)

rows.Check: No orphan nodes (every non-L7 has valid parent)

rows.Result: PASS — ~52 units (1 L7 + 9 L6 + 16 L5 + 26 L4)

units.description: Convert utility feed to stable, redundant IT-grade power at the rack

units.description: (Horizontal) Monitor and optimize PUE, WUE, carbon across all core steps

units.description: Mechanical cooling of primary chilled-water loop

units.name: Heat Rejection Unit
units.description: Cooling tower or dry cooler rejecting heat to ambient

units.name: Coolant Distribution Unit
units.description: Isolates facility loop from IT-grade secondary TCS loop; delivers temperature-controlled coolant to liquid-cooled IT

units.name: In-Rack Liquid Cooling Unit
units.description: Rear-door or sidecar heat exchanger capturing exhaust air into water loop

units.name: Cold Plate Assembly

units.name: CRAH
units.description: Computer-room air handler circulating conditioned air into cold aisle

units.name: Building Management and DCIM
units.description: Supervisory control, telemetry aggregation, alarming for facility and IT

units.name: Storage Array
